mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack triggers recession fears that compress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of periods where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansions in retail sales and housing starts signal broad-based consumer demand growth that boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day forward returns in over 60 percent of triggered instances or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during late-cycle expansions indicates capacity constraints that force central banks to tighten policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive during late-cycle industrial production accelerations or the signal loses predictive power outside expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity and higher corporate revenue growth, which consistently generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 24-month rolling window or payroll growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 0.3 percent signals overheating price pressures that trigger immediate equity sell-offs as investors price in aggressive monetary tightening over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of months where CPIAUCSL monthly growth surpasses 0.3 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth combined with low federal funds rates creates a favorable earnings environment that amplifies equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields negative predictive accuracy or forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline during high-GDP low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll growth alongside falling unemployment during post-shock recovery phases indicates robust labor market normalization that sustains positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns during recovery periods or produces negative returns in over 55 percent of cases.
